hook.text: Caffeine improves focus. Music improves mood. Together, they cause anxiety. That's an interaction.

buildup.text: An interaction occurs when the effect of one factor depends on the level of another factor.

discovery.text: If lines on an interaction plot are not parallel, an interaction is present. Crossing lines signal a strong one.

twist.text: Ignoring interactions leads to misleading conclusions. A drug may 'work' overall but fail in one subgroup completely.

climax.text: Interactions are everywhere: diet × exercise, temperature × humidity, dosage × age. The world doesn't add up neatly.

punchline.text: Factors don't just add. They multiply, cancel, or reverse. Always check.

quiz.question: What does an interaction effect mean in a factorial experiment?
options: The effect of one factor depends on the level of another factor
Both factors have the same effect
Neither factor has any effect on its own
